overview: Sultai Control in Standard leverages three-color synergy to outlast opponents through superior card advantage and resource denial. By utilizing draw engines like Flow State and Rakshasa's Bargain alongside land acceleration from Ancient Cornucopia, the deck stabilizes early and transitions into a resilient late-game. The goal is to control the board with interaction while gradually building an insurmountable hand size and mana base.
win_conditions: Outdrawing the opponent with Rakshasa's Bargain and Flow State
Stabilizing with Ancient Cornucopia to cast late-game threats
Winning through attrition and board control

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction or draw spells
Hands containing Ancient Cornucopia or dual lands
Hands with at least two lands and a one-drop
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including duals or mana rocks), at least one early interaction (Rakshasa's Bargain, discard, or cheap removal), and a Flow State or Ancient Cornucopia if possible.
Prioritize keeping hands that can stabilize turn 1-2 while setting up the late-game engine.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only lands or only high-cost cards
Hands lacking early answers or card draw
Hands with three or more lands
Mulligan to hands with only one land, no early answers, or excessive high-cost spells.

early_game: Play lands and Ancient Cornucopia to accelerate mana
Cast Flow State or Rakshasa's Bargain to draw into answers
Hold up interaction for opponent's early threats
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast Rakshasa's Bargain to disrupt an opponent's key play or draw into a better hand.
Turn 2: Develop your mana base, cast Ancient Cornucopia for early card advantage/mana, or play efficient removal/threats.
Sequence lands to ensure blue/black/green availability by turn 3 for key spells like counterspells or discard.
mid_game: Use extra cards to maintain board control
Search for basics with Cornucopia to fix mana
Trade efficiently while preserving key removal
Preserve mana for interaction until turn 4-5, then commit to board development.
Use Rakshasa's Bargain efficiently to maintain hand size without overcommitting.
Leverage Ancient Cornucopia's sacrifice for card draw when hand is full or mana is saturated.
Protect Flow State in the graveyard
it is your primary win condition engine.
Hold back on committing all threats until you have a clear answer to the opponent's primary win condition.
late_game: Lean on card advantage to outvalue opponents
Cast win conditions or finish with combat tricks
Maintain hand size to pressure opponent's resources
Flow State recursion engine, often paired with Phyrexian Unlife or a combo finisher, plus Ancient Cornucopia for infinite card draw/mana in some configurations.
Close the game by replaying high-impact spells or combo pieces from the graveyard.
Go all-in when you have Flow State online, sufficient mana to replay key spells, and no immediate threat to your life total.
Commit when the opponent has no answers to your recursion or when you can stabilize and out-value them.

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.role: Instant-speed card draw and tempo tool
key_cards.notes: Cast for one mana when possible to maximize efficiency; hold up for key opponent spells or use to dig into late-game answers.

key_cards.name: Ancient Cornucopia
key_cards.notes: Play early to search for missing basics; prioritize untapped lands when possible to maintain tempo, but accept tapped lands to secure three-color consistency.

key_cards.name: Rakshasa's Bargain
key_cards.notes: Use to recover from board wipes or dig for win conditions; manage life total carefully and pair with healing or life gain if available.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting Flow State before Rakshasa's Bargain to maximize hand size
Use Ancient Cornucopia before playing high-cost spells to ensure mana availability
Hold up interaction until the opponent commits to a threat
Draw into answers before committing to combat tricks
protect it from discard and exile effects.
Ancient Cornucopia's sacrifice timing is crucial—do not sacrifice prematurely.
Rakshasa's Bargain is best used to disrupt key turns or refill hand when low.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without recursion online.
Watch for opponent sideboard cards like Rest in Peace or Leyline of the Void.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and life total management; use Rakshasa's Bargain cautiously to avoid lethal damage.
Race for board control while stabilizing with draw spells; use Cornucopia to outpace their mana curve.
Race for card advantage and tempo; use Flow State to disrupt their game plan and dig for win conditions.
Use multiple low-cost spells to force counters, then resolve key threats. Employ flashback/graveyard recursion to bypass counters. Use Ancient Cornucopia to dig for answers or combo pieces.
Prioritize early interaction to disrupt mana development. Use counterspells and discard to slow them down. Leverage Ancient Cornucopia for card advantage to out-value their acceleration.
Win the resource war. Prioritize card advantage, efficient removal, and graveyard protection. Use Rakshasa's Bargain to out-draw them. Protect Flow State and disrupt their recursion.

common_threats: Watch for opponent mana development (especially Izzet's ramp/mana rocks) and graveyard activity.
Note when they start casting high-cost spells or combo pieces.
Track their hand size after Rakshasa's Bargain or discard effects to anticipate their next play.
